QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_15

question.text: The DICGC (Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation) insures bank deposits up to:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** DICGC insures each depositor up to Rs.5,00,000 per bank. This includes all deposits (savings, fixed, recurring, current) and interest accrued, held across all branches of a given bank. **The Trap:** Rs.1,00,000 was the old limit. It was enhanced to Rs.5,00,000 in February 2020. Many study materials still show the old figure. **Cross-Exam Context:** DICGC coverage is tested in Series XII (Securities Markets) and XVII. Compare with NPS where there is no deposit insurance but assets are held by NPS Trust. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise clients to spread large deposits across multiple banks if total deposits exceed Rs.5 lakh per bank, to maximize DICGC coverage.
